definition: Used to emphasize the truth or facts of a situation.
For a short time; in a few words.
In an honest and straightforward way; frankly.
Admit that something is true or valid after first denying or resisting it.
In an open, honest, and direct manner.
In a truthful, fair, or honorable way.
Used to emphasize a statement or response confirming something already suggested.
Say something abruptly, especially as an aside or interruption.
Come between so as to prevent or alter a result or course of events.
Without special intervention; in a natural manner.
In a way that demonstrates a sensible and practical idea of what can be achieved or expected.
Say something as a comment; mention.
In a way that relates to the theory of a subject or area of study rather than its practical application.
